name: Chocolate Diamonds from Death by Chocolate
recipeIngredient: ½ lb Unsalted butter (2 tbsp melted)
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare two 10x15-inch baking sheets by coating the bottom and sides with melted butter. Line each sheet with parchment paper and coat lightly again. Set aside.
2. Preheat oven to 325°F (160°C).
3. In a double boiler over medium heat, combine remaining butter and semisweet chocolate. Cover tightly and let warm for 10-12 minutes. Remove from heat, uncover, and stir until smooth. Keep at room temperature.
4. In an electric mixer with paddle attachment, beat egg yolks and sugar on high until lemon-colored and slightly thickened (about 4 minutes). Scrape sides and continue beating for another 2 minutes.
5. In a dry bowl, beat egg whites with whip attachment until stiff but not dry.
6. Using a rubber spatula, fold chocolate mixture into egg yolk mixture. Add ¼ of the egg white mixture to the chocolate and gently fold in. Incorporate remaining egg whites without white streaks.
7. Divide batter between prepared pans and smooth top. Bake on top and middle racks for 20-22 minutes, rotating sheets halfway through. Let cool for 30 minutes.
8. Toast hazelnuts in a 350°F (175°C) oven for 10-15 minutes until lightly toasted. Rub nuts in kitchen towel to shell them. Chop into 1/8-inch pieces.
9. Invert one cake layer onto a cutting board larger than the pan and refrigerate for 30 minutes. Keep remaining cake at room temperature.
10. For ganache, heat cream, butter, and sugar in a saucepan until sugar dissolves. Bring to boil, then pour over chocolate in bowl. Let stand 6-7 minutes before stirring until smooth. Combine with chopped hazelnuts. Refrigerate used portion, keep remainder at room temperature for filling.
11. For raspberry filling, puree raspberries and sugar in food processor (12-15 seconds). Strain mixture through fine mesh sieve over bowl. Cover and refrigerate until ready.
12. Assemble: Spread raspberry puree on cooled cake layer. Top with hazelnut ganache. Invert second cake layer onto ganache, remove parchment paper, press layers together, and place in freezer for 1 hour.
13. Remove from freezer and trim edges to create 9-inch x 13-½ inch rectangle. Cut widthwise into 9 strips, trimming ends diagonally, then cut each strip crosswise into diamonds.
